name: Falset i monte de las Torres - Circular by the Priorat
name_original: Falset i monte de las Torres - Circular por el Priorat
region_intro: Hiking around Falset offers access to a diverse landscape characterized by mountainous terrain, extensive vineyards, and olive groves. The region features significant natural areas such as the Serra de Montsant Natural Park, known for its sheer cliffs and rocky crags, and the Serra de Llaberia with its imposing limestone formations. Crystal-clear rivers have carved impressive ravines, adding to the varied topography suitable for outdoor exploration. Best hiking trails around Falset The most popular hiking route is Falset i monte de las Torres - Circular by the Priorat, a 4.5 miles (7.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 8 minutes to complete. This circular route explores vineyards and hillsides, offering views of the Priorat region. Another top favourite among local hikers is Font de les Amades – Cave loop from Falset, a moderate 6.2 miles (10.0 km) path. This trail winds through varied terrain, including ravines and rocky areas. Local hikers also love the Cave – Falset loop from Falset, a 5.9 miles (9.5 km) trail leading through the local landscape with views of Falset and surrounding vineyards, often completed in about 2 hours 57 minutes. Hiking around Falset is defined by mountainous terrain, extensive vineyards, and limestone cliffs. The network offers options for different ability levels, from gentle walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Falset are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 214 reviews. More than 1000 hikers have used komoot to explore Falset's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of hiking trails can I expect around Falset?
A: Falset offers a diverse landscape for hiking, characterized by mountainous terrain, extensive vineyards, and olive groves. You'll find trails winding through significant natural areas like the Serra de Montsant Natural Park, known for its sheer cliffs and rocky crags, and the Serra de Llaberia with its imposing limestone formations. Crystal-clear rivers have carved impressive ravines, adding to the varied topography.

Q: Are there any easy hiking routes suitable for beginners or families in Falset?
A: Yes, Falset has several easy routes. For a shorter, less strenuous option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10419586" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cave – View of Falset loop from Falset&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 2.4 miles (3.9 km) long. Another easy choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23756590" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Falset – Cave loop from Falset&lt;/a&gt;, covering about 1.6 miles (2.6 km).

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in the Falset area?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Falset are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Popular circular options include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952544321/falset-i-monte-de-las-torres-circular-by-the-priorat" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Falset i monte de las Torres - Circular by the Priorat&lt;/a&gt;, which explores vineyards and hillsides, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1675643" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font de les Amades – Cave loop from Falset&lt;/a&gt;, winding through varied terrain including ravines.

Q: What are some notable viewpoints or scenic spots along the hiking trails?
A: The region is rich in stunning vistas. The Serra de Montsant Natural Park offers spectacular panoramic views that encompass vineyards, nearby mountains, and even the Mediterranean Sea from its plateau. The Serra de Llaberia also features imposing limestone cliffs that provide breathtaking viewpoints across the Priorat landscape. Many trails through the vineyards offer picturesque scenery.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Falset?
A: The routes in Falset are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 214 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from vineyards to rocky areas, and the well-maintained paths that offer a mix of challenges and scenic beauty.

Q: Are there any historical sites or landmarks to see while hiking around Falset?
A: Yes, the region is dotted with historical and cultural points of interest. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/305718" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Escaladei Charterhouse&lt;/a&gt;, a historic monastery, or visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/226370" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mola Castle, Colldejou&lt;/a&gt;. The Falset area also preserves an invaluable heritage of drystone constructions, with dedicated paths showcasing these unique architectural elements built by farmers over generations.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Falset?
A: Falset is suitable for hiking for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for exploring the trails, with lush landscapes in spring and vibrant colors during the grape harvest season in autumn. Winters are mild, making it possible to hike, while summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended.

Q: Are there any trails that pass through the famous vineyards of Priorat?
A: Absolutely. The region's iconic agricultural landscape is shaped by centuries of winemaking, and numerous paths wind directly through these terraced vineyards and olive groves. Many routes offer opportunities to experience the unique terroir of DOQ Priorat and DO Montsant up close, providing picturesque routes for walkers.

Q: Is it possible to reach hiking trailheads in Falset using public transport?
A: Falset is a regional hub, and while specific trailheads might require a short walk or local taxi, the town itself is accessible by public transport. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for connections to smaller villages or specific starting points for trails further afield.

Q: Are there options for parking near the hiking routes in Falset?
A: Falset, as a town, offers various parking options. For many routes starting directly from Falset, you can typically find street parking or designated public parking areas. For trails located outside the main town, smaller villages or specific trailheads often have limited parking spaces available.

Q: Are there any trails that feature rivers or ravines?
A: Yes, crystal-clear rivers, such as the Montsant River, have carved out impressive ravines over millennia, adding a dramatic dimension to the natural scenery. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6930799" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cave – Falset loop from Falset&lt;/a&gt; wind through varied terrain, including rocky areas and ravines, offering a different kind of natural beauty.

Q: Can I hike with my dog on the trails around Falset?
A: Many trails in the Falset region are suitable for hiking with dogs, especially those in natural areas and vineyards. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in protected areas like the Serra de Montsant Natural Park, to protect wildlife and respect other hikers. Always carry water for your dog and clean up after them.
tips: Falset Castle is one of the emblems of the Priorat, a wine region with a very beautiful town.
Panoramic view of the town of Falset and its surroundings

The hermitage of Sant Gregori is built under the reddish sandstone rocks of the mountain near the municipality of Falset. In front of the hermitage there is a balcony with an excellent viewpoint of the Falset valley. There is also a picnic area with tables very close by.
